contribution_description: [CONTRIBUTION EVENT] - Type: Time (Minutes) - Amount: 30 - Description: capoeira.agroverse.shop mobile optimization (index + shared nav across all 4 pages) Pull requests (GitHub evidence): - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/14 Details: Mobile pass on https://capoeira.agroverse.shop/ — Gary opened the page and asked to make it work better on phones. Audited the existing CSS, found half a dozen specific issues at <600px, fixed them in one shared CSS file (covers all four pages: index / practice / library / transparency) plus a new shared nav.js for the hamburger. CSS fixes (assets/css/styles.css): - Extended the existing 600px breakpoint with the bits that were missing: .donate-cta padding (was 4rem fixed), .section-heading margin-bottom (was 3rem on mobile), .narrative typography, .photo-item img height. The breakpoint touched .section but not its sibling classes. - .donate-amount uses flex: 1 1 80px so the 5 amount buttons wrap to a clean 3+2 grid on phones instead of an unbalanced 4+1 with one tiny button stranded on its own row. Was the most visible mobile glitch. - .hero-buttons .btn use flex: 1 1 auto with min-width 140px so the two hero CTAs stack as full-width buttons rather than awkward partial-width pairs at narrow widths. - .hero-content h1 br { display: none } at <600px so the 'Capoeira Practice [br] Tribo Bahia Mirim' headline wraps naturally instead of always breaking at the same forced point regardless of viewport. - Stat boxes / footer / narrative padding all tightened. - New 480px breakpoint for tight-screen header: shrinks logo image 48px to 36px, drops nav padding. Without this the logo + image + hamburger crowd each other on small phones. - New .move-table-wrapper utility (overflow-x: auto + iOS momentum scroll) for any wide table on narrow screens. Applied on library.html so the 5-column move table can scroll horizontally instead of blowing out the viewport. JS (new assets/js/nav.js, replaces inline onclick on all 4 pages): - Mobile nav hamburger now toggles aria-expanded on the button, closes on tap outside the menu, closes on ESC (and returns focus to the hamburger), closes on tapping any link inside the menu (so it doesn't stay open while the new page loads). Previously was just classList.toggle with no aria state, no outside-click close, no ESC close — would stay open until the user re-tapped the hamburger. - Markup contract:
rubric: 100TDG For every 1 hour of human effort
contribution_type: 100TDG For every 1 hour of human effort
